docs.sui.io Analytics

2026-04-14 to 2026-04-20 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260421T170640Z

Summary Traffic Top Pages Referrals Top Referrer URLs Page↔Theme Takeaways Topics User Engagement Sprint
Overview

Executive Summary

Kapa Questions
767
Themes Identified
12
Visitors (7d)
4,152
Represents roughly 44% of the 14-day total (9,448), suggesting relatively stable week-over-week traffic with no sharp spike or drop.
Pageviews (7d)
12,326
Averages ~1,761 pageviews/day. Monday Apr 20 alone contributed 2,292 — the week's peak — indicating strong weekday demand.
Bounce Rate (7d)
62%
Slightly better than the 14d (65%) and 30d (65%) averages, suggesting the most recent week attracted slightly more engaged visitors.
Views per Visit (7d)
2.64
Marginally higher than the 14d (2.60) and 30d (2.56) figures, indicating a modest but consistent upward trend in content depth per session.
Avg. Visit Duration (7d)
157 seconds
Below the 14d average of 168s and 30d of 160s, suggesting recent visitors are slightly quicker in finding what they need or bouncing faster.
Weekend Traffic Drop
448–472 visitors/day
Apr 18–19 (Sat–Sun) saw 40% fewer visitors than peak weekdays, confirming a developer-skewed audience with strong weekday usage patterns.
Developer activity this week was dominated by API migration pressure (JSON-RPC deprecation deadline looming), with Move contract development and TypeScript/PTB SDK usage as the next largest pain clusters. Onboarding and installation pages drive the majority of docs traffic, while the Hello World tutorial shows a concerning 68% bounce rate indicating a critical funnel drop-off point.
Plausible

Traffic Overview

docs.sui.io saw 4,152 unique visitors and 12,326 pageviews over the 7-day period (Apr 14–20, 2026). Engagement is moderate with 2.64 views per visit, a 62% bounce rate, and an average session duration of 157 seconds. Traffic is dominated by direct visits and Google search, with strong interest in onboarding and installation guides. Monday Apr 20 was the busiest day with 708 visitors and 2,292 pageviews, while the weekend (Apr 18–19) saw the expected dip.

Monday Apr 20 Traffic Surge

Apr 20 recorded the week's highest visitors (708) and pageviews (2,292) with the lowest bounce rate (55%). This may correlate with a content release, announcement, or community event on or around that date.

Dual-path Install Guide Fragmentation

Both /getting-started/onboarding/sui-install (363 visitors) and /guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install (143 visitors) are receiving significant traffic. The legacy path is still highly active, suggesting redirects are incomplete and SEO link equity is split.

AI Chatbot Referrals Emerging

ChatGPT (25 visitors) and Claude (8 visitors) together sent 33 visitors this week. While small in absolute terms, this is a growing channel as AI assistants increasingly cite and link to official developer documentation.

Onboarding Funnel Engagement Gradient

The onboarding hub (9% bounce) → install (41%) → get-coins (42%) → hello-world (68%) shows increasing drop-off through the funnel. The Hello World tutorial has the highest bounce in the sequence, flagging it as the key point to optimize for conversion.

DeepBook DeFi Developer Interest

Both /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook (107 visitors, 20% bounce) and referrals from deepbook.tech (94 visitors) confirm strong, targeted DeFi developer interest in DeepBook integration content this week.

Weekend Traffic Dip Confirms Developer Audience

Traffic drops ~36% on weekends (448–472 visitors/day) versus weekdays (617–708), consistent with a professional developer audience accessing docs during work hours rather than a consumer or hobbyist base.

Plausible

Top 20 Pages

# Page Insight
1 / Top page with 1,121 visitors and 1,459 pageviews. Low 23% bounce rate and 69s time-on-page suggest it effectively routes users deeper into the docs.
2 /getting-started/onboarding/sui-install 363 visitors and 476 pageviews — the most-visited non-home page. 41% bounce rate and 58s on-page show strong intent; installation is a primary developer entry point.
3 /sui-api-ref 136 visitors but 260 pageviews (1.9x ratio), indicating return visits and deep reference browsing. Low 37% bounce rate confirms high utility for active developers.
4 /guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install 143 visitors — old path for install guide still receiving significant traffic alongside the new /getting-started path, suggesting incomplete redirect coverage.
5 /getting-started/onboarding/get-coins 132 visitors with 42% bounce rate. Part of the core onboarding funnel; users progressing from install to acquiring coins.
6 /getting-started/onboarding 157 visitors with a remarkably low 9% bounce rate, indicating this hub page successfully channels users into the onboarding flow.
7 /getting-started/onboarding/hello-world 118 visitors with a high 68% bounce rate, suggesting many users attempt the Hello World tutorial but drop off — a potential content or UX improvement opportunity.
8 /getting-started/tooling 100 visitors, 167 pageviews, low 25% bounce rate. Tooling reference is well-used and sticky, likely bookmarked or revisited frequently.
9 /references/fullnode-protocol 89 visitors with a low 22% bounce rate and 25s on-page. Targeted operator/developer audience finding specific protocol reference quickly.
10 /getting-started 109 visitors, 9% bounce rate — another strong funnel hub. Very low bounce confirms it's driving users deeper into the getting-started content tree.
11 /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook 107 visitors, 20% bounce rate. DeepBook docs are attracting a focused DeFi developer audience with high engagement.
12 /references/sui-api 108 visitors and 144 pageviews. API reference is a steady staple for developers integrating with Sui.
13 /develop/sui-architecture/tokenomics-overview 112 visitors but 50% bounce rate. Tokenomics content draws interest but may not satisfy user intent — worth reviewing for depth and clarity.
14 /getting-started/onboarding/configure-sui-client 82 visitors, low 19% bounce rate. Client configuration is a natural next step after install and users engage well with this content.
15 /getting-started/onboarding/ 73 visitors — trailing slash variant receiving separate traffic from the non-slash version; could be consolidated to avoid split analytics.
16 /guides 82 visitors, 17% bounce rate. The guides index page is an effective navigator with very low abandonment.
17 /develop/write-move/sui-move-concepts 91 visitors, 44% bounce rate. Move concepts attract intermediate developers; moderate bounce suggests mixed satisfaction with content depth.
18 /getting-started/onboarding/get-address 64 visitors, 24% bounce rate, 56s on-page. Step in the onboarding funnel with solid engagement — users are following through the flow.
19 /develop/sui-architecture/ 72 visitors, 18% bounce rate. Architecture overview is a low-bounce reference page, indicating developers use it as a navigation hub.
20 /develop/accessing-data/grpc 56 visitors with a notably high 54% bounce rate but 73s on-page. Users spend time here but still bounce — content may need clearer next steps or deeper examples.
Plausible

Referral Sources

Source Type Insight
Direct / None Organic 1,955 visitors — the largest single source (47% of tracked traffic). High direct traffic suggests a loyal returning developer base or bookmark-heavy audience.
Google Organic 1,125 visitors with a 55% bounce rate and 214s avg duration — the highest quality search traffic. SEO is working well for capturing developer intent.
sui.io Organic 485 visitors referred from the main Sui website. Strong internal ecosystem cross-linking is effectively funneling users to docs.
Bing Organic 208 visitors with 80% bounce rate — highest of all sources. Bing traffic is significantly lower quality than Google, possibly mismatched search intent.
deepbook.tech Organic 94 visitors with 46% bounce and 197s duration. DeepBook's own site is actively sending engaged DeFi developers to the Sui docs.
docs.sui.io Organic 78 visitors self-referrals — likely internal page links or iframe-related tracking artifacts. 54% bounce but 362s duration suggests deep reading sessions.
blog.sui.io Organic 42 visitors from the Sui blog, including a specific post on Move Registry Interoperability (30 visitors). Blog-to-docs pipeline is functioning.
GitHub Organic 32 visitors from GitHub with 50% bounce rate. Developer-originated traffic from repo READMEs and issues, though relatively small volume.
Baidu Organic 28 visitors with 93% bounce rate and 0s duration — essentially no engagement. Chinese search traffic is not finding relevant content; may warrant investigation.
chatgpt.com AI / Chatbot 25 visitors referred by ChatGPT. AI assistant is actively directing users to Sui docs, a growing and notable channel for developer documentation discovery.
DuckDuckGo Organic 20 visitors with 60% bounce rate. Privacy-focused search users are a small but present segment; content quality for these queries may need improvement.
suiplay.sui.io Organic 18 visitors from SuiPlay — the gaming/consumer product is sending users to docs, likely developers building on the platform.
Twitter Organic 17 visitors with 68% bounce rate and 158s duration. Social media referrals are modest; engaged users do read content when they arrive.
Facebook Organic 16 visitors with 50% bounce and 132s duration. Small but present social channel, likely from community group posts.
evefrontier.com Organic 11 visitors from Eve Frontier — a game built on Sui. Developer traffic from ecosystem game projects is a meaningful signal of builder activity.
luma Organic 10 visitors but 460s avg duration — the highest of all sources. Event attendees from Luma-hosted Sui events are highly engaged when they reach docs.
claude.ai AI / Chatbot 8 visitors from Claude. Along with ChatGPT, AI assistants are becoming a measurable referral channel for Sui docs, totaling 33 visitors this week.
Yahoo! Organic 8 visitors with 75% bounce rate and near-zero duration. Yahoo search traffic quality is very low; not a meaningful channel.
okg-block.sg.larksuite.com Organic 7 visitors from a Lark (enterprise messaging) workspace in Singapore — indicates organized team-based doc sharing in the Asian developer community.
Yandex Organic 6 visitors with 33% bounce and 77s duration. Small but relatively engaged Russian-language search traffic.
AI Referral Detail

chatgpt.com

25 visitors referred this week; ChatGPT is actively citing Sui docs as an authoritative source, making doc accuracy critical for AI-mediated discovery.

claude.ai

8 visitors referred this week; combined with ChatGPT, AI assistants now account for 33 Sui docs visitors, a nascent but growing channel worth optimizing for.

Cross-Signal

Page ↔ Kapa Theme Correlations

High-traffic pages mapped to the Kapa themes they correspond to.

Page Related Kapa Theme Insight
/develop/accessing-data/grpc gRPC and GraphQL API Usage & Migration 54% bounce despite 73s on-page signals unresolved gRPC questions driving chatbot escalation.
/getting-started/onboarding/hello-world Move Smart Contract Development 68% bounce aligns with high uncertain_count (47) in Move fundamentals questions.
/guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install Package Deployment and Upgrades Legacy install path still active; duplicate traffic splitting SEO equity across two URLs.
/getting-started/onboarding/sui-install Node and Infrastructure Operations 363 visitors suggest CLI install is a primary pain point matching node setup questions.
/onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook Data Querying and Indexing 107 visitors with 20% bounce correlates with 38 DeepBook data querying questions this week.
/develop/write-move/sui-move-concepts Move Smart Contract Development 44% bounce reflects mixed satisfaction matching 47 uncertain Move concept questions.
/develop/sui-architecture/tokenomics-overview Staking and Network Fundamentals 50% bounce suggests tokenomics page is not satisfying staking and economics questions.
/references/sui-api gRPC and GraphQL API Usage & Migration Steady API reference traffic confirms developers actively cross-referencing during JSON-RPC migration.
Analysis

Notable Takeaways

Hello World is the highest-bounce page in the onboarding funnel

68% bounce rate vs 9% for the onboarding hub and 41% for the install page

The Hello World tutorial is the single biggest conversion failure in the onboarding sequence and warrants immediate content intervention.

Legacy install path is splitting significant traffic

143 visitors on /guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install alongside 363 on the new path

Incomplete redirects are fragmenting SEO equity and creating a confusing dual-path experience for new developers.

gRPC API migration is the largest unanswered question cluster

47 uncertain out of 132 total gRPC/GraphQL questions (36% uncertainty rate)

Nearly two in five gRPC migration questions go unanswered by the bot, indicating critical documentation gaps ahead of the July 31, 2026 JSON-RPC deprecation deadline.

Move contract fundamentals have the second-highest uncertainty rate

47 uncertain out of 121 total Move smart contract questions

Despite being the second-largest theme, Move fundamentals have a high bot failure rate suggesting canonical runnable examples are missing from docs.

Kapa

Developer Topics

This week saw extremely high developer activity across gRPC/GraphQL API migration, Move contract development, TypeScript/PTB SDK usage, and package deployment workflows. The largest question clusters centered on API migration from JSON-RPC, PTB construction patterns, and Move smart contract fundamentals. Questions grouped by specific topic, with confident vs uncertain answer breakdown.

Q: Can a move function establish a synchronous connection to a target address and/or send a message to that address?

Q: what is the sui mainnet full node url

Q: how can i get a sui address from a public key
A: A Sui address is derived by hashing the **signature scheme flag byte concatenated with the public key bytes** (`flag || pubkey`) using the **BLAKE2b** (256-bit output) hashing function. The result is …

Q: 那为什么使用官方grpc端点可以正常转账而本地部署的grpc节点不行？

#336 1 question

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: 并不是落后checkpoint，而是同步后和链上状态不一致，那么我觉得应该是某个接受的checkpoint数据有问题，那么怎么快速恢复呢
A: 你的判断很有道理——如果节点已经同步到最新 checkpoint，但本地状态与链上不一致，很可能是某个 checkpoint 数据在接收或执行时出现了问题，导致本地数据库处于不一致状态。 根据知识库中的信息，**最快的恢复方式仍然是从快照重建**，因为这可以绕过有问题的本地数据库状态，直接恢复到一个经过协议验证的干净状态。 ## 推荐：使用 Formal Snapshot 恢复 Formal…

A: 是的，你的思路是正确的。对于多签账户升级包，使用 `--serialize-unsigned-transaction` 生成未签名的交易字节，然后由各签名方分别签名，最后合并执行，这是标准的离线签名流程。[[Offline Signing](https://docs.sui.io/guides/developer/transactions/transaction-auth/offline-sign…

#478 1 question

Move Copy Ability

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: Can you explain what does the copy ability do?
A: ## The `copy` Ability in Sui Move The `copy` ability allows values of a type to be **duplicated or copied**. Without it, Move types are linear by default — meaning they can only be moved, not copied.…

Sprint Planning

Recommended Actions

Doc Actions
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Publish JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL Side-by-Side Migration Reference

New dedicated migration guide covering all common JSON-RPC operations with gRPC and GraphQL equivalents including pagination, BCS parsing, and staking data.

47 unanswered gRPC questions and July 31 2026 deprecation deadline make this the single highest-impact doc gap.

↑ Reduce gRPC uncertain_count and eliminate repeated escalation patterns visible across 132 questions.
The docs have separate gRPC and GraphQL sections (https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/grpc/index.md, https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/graphql/index.md) but no dedicated side-by-side JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL migration reference covering pagination, BCS parsing, and staking data equivalents.
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Rewrite the Hello World Tutorial with Reduced Drop-off

Full revision of /getting-started/onboarding/hello-world including clearer prerequisites, inline error handling, and explicit next-step links.

68% bounce rate is the highest in the onboarding funnel and represents the primary conversion failure point.

↑ Improve onboarding funnel completion rate and reduce the gap between install page engagement and tutorial engagement.
The Hello World page exists at https://docs.sui.io/getting-started/onboarding/hello-world.md but the recommendation for clearer prerequisites, inline error handling, and explicit next-step links has not been addressed in the listed content structure.
high Documentation Partial

Consolidate Dual Install Guide Paths with Permanent Redirects

Implement 301 redirects from /guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install to /getting-started/onboarding/sui-install and audit all internal links.

143 visitors weekly on the legacy path are receiving a fragmented experience and splitting SEO equity.

↑ Consolidate traffic, fix analytics fragmentation, and ensure all developers reach the canonical up-to-date install guide.
The install guide appears to exist only at https://docs.sui.io/getting-started/onboarding/sui-install.md with no evidence of the old /guides/developer/getting-started/sui-install path or documented 301 redirects auditing internal links.
medium Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Add Move Versioned Shared Object and Upgrade Patterns Cookbook

New page with canonical runnable examples for package versioning, UpgradeCap management, Published.toml workflow, and upgrade compatibility error resolution.

31 uncertain upgrade questions and 47 uncertain Move questions share root cause of missing concrete code examples.

↑ Reduce bot uncertainty rate for package deployment and Move development themes, currently the two largest uncertain clusters.
While versioning pages exist at https://docs.sui.io/develop/publish-upgrade-packages/versioning.md and https://docs.sui.io/develop/objects/versioning.md, there is no dedicated cookbook page with canonical runnable examples for UpgradeCap management, Published.toml workflow, and upgrade compatibility error resolution.
Tooling Actions
high Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Improve sui client upgrade and publish CLI Error Messages for Package Management Failures

Improve error output for UpgradeCap mismatches, Published.toml inconsistencies, and unpublished dependency failures to include specific remediation hints.

Multiple classified questions show developers confused by opaque upgrade errors that the bot partially answers but cannot fully resolve.

↑ Reduce support load for package deployment theme and enable developers to self-diagnose upgrade failures without documentation lookup.
The docs cover upgrade and publish concepts at https://docs.sui.io/develop/publish-upgrade-packages/upgrade.md but there is no documentation of improved CLI error messages with remediation hints for UpgradeCap mismatches or Published.toml inconsistencies.
high Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Add Transaction Resolution Support or Fallback Hint to SuiGrpcClient

Fix or document the SuiGrpcClient limitation where Transaction.build passes a gRPC client that lacks resolution support, causing runtime errors with no actionable guidance.

Classified question shows developers hitting 'Transaction resolution is not supported with the GRPC client' with no clear workaround in current tooling or error output.

↑ Unblock TypeScript developers migrating from JSON-RPC to gRPC who hit this error as their first SDK integration step.
The gRPC documentation at https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/grpc/using-grpc.md does not appear to document the SuiGrpcClient limitation regarding Transaction.build resolution support or provide fallback guidance.
DX Actions
medium Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Add Interactive PTB Construction Debugger to Sui CLI

New sui client ptb --dry-run --verbose mode that shows argument resolution steps, command index mapping, and type-match validation before execution.

38 uncertain PTB questions and classified CommandArgumentError patterns show developers cannot diagnose arg_idx failures without manual inspection.

↑ Give developers a self-service way to diagnose PTB construction errors that currently require chatbot or forum escalation.
The PTB CLI reference exists at https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/ptb.md but there is no documented --dry-run --verbose mode showing argument resolution steps, command index mapping, or type-match validation.
medium Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Build a gRPC Migration Code Scanner for JSON-RPC Callsites

New CLI tool or SDK utility that scans a TypeScript or Rust codebase for deprecated JSON-RPC method calls and outputs the recommended gRPC or GraphQL equivalent with code snippets.

132 gRPC migration questions and the July 2026 deadline mean thousands of developers need to audit large codebases with no automated assistance.

↑ Dramatically accelerate ecosystem migration speed and reduce the volume of repetitive migration questions hitting the chatbot and support channels.
No CLI tool or SDK utility for scanning codebases for deprecated JSON-RPC callsites and suggesting gRPC/GraphQL equivalents is documented anywhere in the site pages provided.
docs.sui.io Docs Report · 2026-04-14 to 2026-04-20 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260421T170640Z
